title: hibbertia porcata
intro: Hibbertia porcata, also known as Yellow Cow-wheat, is a species of flowering plant in the family Dilleniaceae. It has been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine, particularly by the Aboriginal people of the Kimberley region, for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Herbal preparations of Hibbertia porcata have been traditionally used in the form of infusions, decoctions, and poultices, although further research is needed to fully understand its potential medicinal uses and benefits.
